Where to Start

5 focused changes that fix most of what's holding this page back. Tackle these first — everything else can wait.

[01]

Redesign the hero — add headline, H1, and primary CTA

HIGH
Fixes 6 findings across 3 clustersHeadlines & ButtonsSearch FindabilityTrust & Credibility

The desktop hero is a full-bleed car photo with overlaid category quick-links but no headline, no H1, no subheadline, and no primary CTA button. The five category buttons compete at equal visual weight with no hierarchy. The title tag ("AWDMods – AWDMods") and empty meta description compound the problem for search.

▸Do this:Add a headline + subheadline block in the left half of the hero (where 80% of desktop fixations land), a primary "Shop All Parts" CTA button at the Gutenberg terminal position (bottom-right), and set the Shopify title/meta description to match. Place the headline in an H1 tag.
Underlying findings

  • Headlines & Buttonsvisual-cta F-06→
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-01→
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-02→
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-03→
[02]

Build review volume past the 5-review threshold

HIGH
Fixes 3 findings across 2 clustersTrust & CredibilityBrowse & Search

Same as mobile: product cards show 5.0/5.0 from 2 reviews. Most featured products have zero reviews visible. The 270% conversion lift from crossing 5 reviews is the single highest-leverage trust action.

▸Do this:Launch a post-purchase review email campaign targeting products with fewer than 5 reviews.
Underlying findings
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-02→
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-03→
  • Browse & Searchcategory-navigation F-07→
[03]

Add a trust strip below the hero

HIGH
Fixes 4 findings across 1 clusterTrust & Credibility

No trust signals above the fold on desktop. Payment icons are footer-only. No return policy, guarantee, or "Made to Order" explanation visible before scrolling. Contact info buried.

▸Do this:Add one row below the hero: aggregate review count + payment icons + "30-Day Returns" + "Made to Order = 3-5 day lead time."
Underlying findings
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-01→
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-04→
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-05→
  • Trust & Credibilitytrust-credibility F-06→
[04]

Fix hero image loading and SEO indexability

HIGH
Fixes 4 findings across 2 clustersMobile ExperienceSearch Findability

Hero image has no preload hint or fetchpriority="high" — the browser discovers it late, delaying loading. No modern image format (WebP/AVIF) delivery confirmed. Images lack width/height attributes causing layout shift. Hero image may be a CSS background — invisible to Google Images.

▸Do this:Add `<link rel="preload">` for the hero image with `fetchpriority="high"`, confirm it's an `<img>` element with descriptive alt text, and add width/height to all images.
Underlying findings
  • Mobile Experiencemobile-performance F-01→
  • Mobile Experiencemobile-performance F-02→
  • Mobile Experiencemobile-performance F-05→
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-07→
[05]

Complete schema markup and SEO metadata

MEDIUM
Fixes 3 findings across 1 clusterSearch Findability

Missing Organization schema (no Knowledge Panel eligibility), no ItemList for Featured Collection, OG/Twitter Card tags collapsed to brand name only with no og:image. Privacy Policy footer link pointing to myshopify.com subdomain.

▸Do this:Add Organization JSON-LD with sameAs social links, add ItemList for featured products, fix OG tags with a 1200×630 branded image, and fix the Privacy Policy link to use awdmods.com domain.
Underlying findings
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-05→
  • Search Findabilitycontent-seo F-09→

Primary Interface Evidence
Diagnostic Insights
1 High
VISUAL

Hero Layout

The hero contains no headline. The entire above-fold area is a car photo with small overlaid category-filter buttons but no text answering "What does this site sell?" A first-time visitor has no written value proposition.

Recommendation

Add a headline and subheadline in the left half of the hero, with sufficient contrast over the hero image. Position in the left half where desktop eye-tracking shows 80% of fixations land.

NNGroup research shows visitors decide whether to stay within 10–20 seconds. A hero with no headline forces the visitor to infer value from an image — slower and less reliable.
Hero Section Psychology Gold
View Source
2 Medium
VISUAL

Trust Above Fold

No trust signal visible above the fold. No review count, star rating, customer count, or credibility indicator.

Recommendation

Add one compact trust signal in the hero section beneath a headline: "★★★★★ 4.8 · 1,200+ reviews."

Stanford Web Credibility research found users assess credibility within seconds from visual cues and visible customer validation.
Hero Section Psychology Gold
View Source
3 High
VISUAL

Primary Cta

The hero has no primary CTA button — only small overlaid category quick-links. The hero occupies the dominant visual area but offers no clear first-action instruction.

Recommendation

Add a single primary CTA button ("Shop All Parts" or "Find Parts for My Vehicle") at the Gutenberg terminal position (bottom-right of the hero text block).

Without a clear primary action, visitors must choose among five equal-weight links — a choice-overload situation linked to lower conversion.
Hero Section Psychology Gold
View Source
4 Medium
VISUAL

Cta Contrast

The near-black CTA buttons blend into the dark hero background. In the multicolumn section they're functional but don't stand out as action elements.

Recommendation

Use a bright accent color for the hero CTA. Test an orange or amber variant for category CTAs to create contrast against dark backgrounds.

CXL meta-analysis found CTA performance is driven by contrast against surroundings, not specific color. Jackson's Art Supplies saw 18.4% conversion increase from CTA differentiation.
CTA Design And Placement Silver
View Source
5 Medium
VISUAL

Visual Hierarchy

Five equally-weighted category CTAs (Performance, Handling, Interior, Exterior, Electronics) share the same button style, color, and size. This creates flat hierarchy where five actions demand attention simultaneously.

Recommendation

Establish primary/secondary hierarchy. Make one the "featured" path or add a primary "Shop All" CTA above them.

When elements share equal weight, users experience micro-paralysis and take longer to act.
Eye Tracking And Scan Patterns Bronze
View Source
6 Medium
VISUAL

Scan Pattern

The hero content is centered with no text in the top-left region — the primary optical area where eyes land first. The value proposition belongs in the left half but the space is empty.

Recommendation

Place primary text content in the left half of the hero. Car image can remain right side.

80% of desktop fixations land on the left half. Leaving it empty means the most-seen area communicates nothing.
Eye Tracking And Scan Patterns Gold
View Source
7 Low
VISUAL

Cta Copy

Generic all-caps labels with weak verb "SHOP" convey no outcome or benefit.

Recommendation

Test stronger action verbs: "Browse Performance Upgrades," "Explore Handling Parts."

NNGroup found generic verbs consistently underperform outcome-oriented verbs.
Headline Copywriting Gold
View Source
8 Low
VISUAL

Whitespace

Category card CTAs have limited surrounding whitespace, reducing visual prominence.

Recommendation

Increase padding inside each card, particularly above/below the CTA button.

Whitespace around CTAs increases perceived importance and click rates.
CTA Design And Placement Bronze
View Source
9 Low
VISUAL

Above Fold Content

The newsletter submit button is a small arrow icon with no text label. The newsletter headline "Join Our Newsletter!" is generic with no stated benefit.

Recommendation

Replace with a labeled button ("Subscribe"). Rewrite headline to include a specific benefit.

Baymard found 73% of users were surprised when a CTA didn't clearly communicate what would happen next.
CTA Design And Placement Gold
View Source
10 High
VISUAL

Trust Above Fold

Zero trust signals above the fold on desktop. No review count, no guarantee, no security badges. First-time visitors have no immediate evidence this is trustworthy.

Recommendation

Add a slim trust bar below the hero or announcement bar with review count, return policy, and payment icons.

Trust badges near conversion points lift conversion 15–32% for unknown brands (Baymard, 4,400+ sessions).
Trust And Credibility Gold
View Source
11 High
VISUAL

Reviews Display

Every rated product shows 5.0/5.0 from only 2 reviews. Perfect score + tiny count triggers skepticism, not confidence.

Recommendation

Until products have 5+ reviews, consider not displaying stars on cards. Prioritize review collection.

Purchase likelihood peaks at 4.0–4.7 stars and falls approaching 5.0 (Spiegel/Northwestern). Products with 5+ reviews convert at 270% higher rates.
Trust And Credibility Gold
View Source
12 High
VISUAL

Review Count

Store-wide review depth is too shallow. Featured products have 2 reviews each; others may have zero.

Recommendation

Launch systematic post-purchase review email campaign targeting products under 5 reviews.

270% conversion lift from 5+ reviews. For expensive auto parts, the multiplier reaches 380%.
Social Proof Patterns Gold
View Source

VISUAL

Trust Badges

Payment icons present in footer but require scrolling past the entire page to see.

12.2% conversion lift from moving trust badges adjacent to the purchase action.

Social Proof Placement

Homepage-level signals answer "Is this a real company with real customers?" Without them, visitors rely on brand recognition alone.

17 High
CODE

Above Fold Content

Title tag is "AWDMods – AWDMods" — brand repeated twice, no keywords, near-certain Google rewrite.

Recommendation

Rewrite to ~55 chars: "AWD Performance Parts & Mods for WRX, STI, Golf R | AWDMods". Front-load keywords.

Google rewrites 61–76% of titles. Optimal 50–60 char window yields 33.3% higher CTR.
Title Formulas SERP Psychology Silver

CODE

Above Fold Content

No meta description. OG description also just "AWDMods". Google auto-generates snippets from page boilerplate.

Write 140–160 char meta description leading with customer benefit and vehicle names. Fix OG/Twitter tags to match.

Crafted descriptions significantly outperform auto-generated snippets. Rich results achieve 58% vs 41% CTR.
Title Formulas SERP Psychology Gold

Visual Hierarchy

No H1 anywhere in the homepage DOM. H2s and H3s exist but no primary heading.

H1/title misalignment is a top trigger for Google title rewrites.
Title Formulas SERP Psychology Silver

CODE

Above Fold Content

Missing Organization schema, ShippingService schema, and SiteLinksSearchBox. No ItemList for Featured Collection.

Organization schema enables Knowledge Panel. ShippingService (Nov 2025) makes the free shipping threshold machine-readable.

BOTH

Hero Layout

Hero image may be a CSS background (not indexable). Alt text unconfirmed. If CSS background, it's invisible to Google Images and Google Lens (4B monthly shopping searches).

Recommendation

Confirm it's an img element. Add descriptive alt text. Host at a stable URL.

CSS background images get zero SEO value. 3,948 ADA lawsuits filed in 2025, 91.5% targeting ecommerce.
Image SEO Alt Text Gold

22 High
BOTH

Visual Hierarchy

Category card images may lack alt text. Only the header logo matched the alt-text selector in DOM extraction.

Missing alt text is an ADA compliance risk and image SEO gap.
Image SEO Alt Text Gold

CODE

Above Fold Content

Privacy Policy links to e1520g-k3.myshopify.com instead of awdmods.com. Creates canonical signal inconsistency and trust issue.

Recommendation

Update to relative path /policies/privacy-policy.

Internal links pointing off-domain pass equity to the wrong domain and send inconsistent canonical signals.

CODE

Above Fold Content

No Product or ItemList schema on homepage for the featured products.

ItemList creates explicit crawl paths. AI shopping agents consume Schema.org markup.

CODE

Above Fold Content

og:title = "AWDMods", og:description = "AWDMods", no og:image. Social shares show no thumbnail.

Missing og:image means social shares show no thumbnail — significantly lower engagement.

CODE

Above Fold Content

No evidence ChatGPT Shopping Sales Channel is enabled. Shopify has native first-class integration (Nov 2025).

Enable in Shopify admin Settings > Sales Channels. Verify product GTINs/MPNs are populated.

AI-referred shoppers show 27% lower bounce rates and 8–10% higher engagement (Adobe Analytics, 2025). Early adoption = first-mover advantage.

27 Medium
BOTH

Above Fold Content

No homepage text block describes AWDMods' offering, vehicles served, or value proposition in crawlable body copy. Vehicle-specific keywords only appear in the newsletter section.

Recommendation

Add a 100–150 word content block between category cards and Featured Collection naming vehicle platforms and key categories.

A homepage with no body copy gives Google minimal content for topical relevance. Vehicle names are high-intent search terms.
Benefit First Descriptions Gold
View Source
28 High
CODE

Image Optimization

No preload hint or fetchpriority="high" for the hero image. Browser discovers and fetches it late in the loading sequence.

Recommendation

Add <link rel="preload" as="image" fetchpriority="high"> in head. Add fetchpriority="high" to the hero img element.

Every 0.1 second improvement increases retail conversion by 8.4% (Deloitte/Google, 30M+ sessions). Vodafone A/B test: 31% LCP improvement = 8% more sales.
Core Web Vitals Gold
View Source
29 Medium
CODE

Image Optimization

No AVIF/WebP delivery confirmed. Standard img elements without picture element wrappers.

Recommendation

Use Shopify's image_url filter with format: 'webp'. Implement picture elements with AVIF/WebP/JPEG fallback.

WebP is 25–34% smaller; AVIF is 45–55% smaller. Smaller images = faster LCP.
Media Performance Optimization Gold
View Source
30 Medium
CODE

Page Load

Main stylesheet loads synchronously as render-blocking. No critical CSS inlining in head. Browser can't paint until full CSS downloads.

Recommendation

Inline critical above-fold CSS (~4-8KB) in a style tag. Defer base.css with media="print" onload="this.media='all'" pattern.

A 1-second delay in above-fold visibility reduces conversion by 7%.
Page Performance Psychology Silver
View Source
31 Medium
CODE

Image Optimization

Images lack explicit width/height attributes — causes layout shift as images load.

Recommendation

Add width and height to every img element. Add CSS img { max-width: 100%; height: auto; }

CLS causes misclicks and erodes trust. Swappie reduced CLS by 91% → 42% mobile revenue increase.
Media Performance Optimization Gold
View Source
32 Medium
CODE

Page Load

Shopify-injected accelerated checkout stylesheet loads synchronously. Full third-party script audit needed.

Recommendation

Run Chrome DevTools Performance audit. Categorize and remove non-revenue-attributable scripts.

Third-party scripts are the leading cause of CWV failures on Shopify stores.
Core Web Vitals Silver
View Source
33 Medium
CODE

Lazy Loading

No Speculation Rules API for collection page prerendering. Ray-Ban saw +156% desktop PDP conversion.

Recommendation

Add speculationrules script for top collection pages with eagerness: "moderate".

Instant page transitions multiply browsing depth and conversion.
Core Web Vitals Silver
View Source
34 Low
CODE

Font Loading

Font preloads present (good) but no font-display: swap or size-adjusted fallback metrics.

Recommendation

Add font-display: swap and size-adjust CSS descriptors to minimize layout shift on font swap.

Font swap without size-adjusted fallbacks causes CLS.
Core Web Vitals Silver
View Source
35 High
BOTH

Price Anchoring

No reference price, MSRP, or "compare at" value on any product card. Shoppers have no anchor to judge value.

Recommendation

Display genuine strikethrough MSRP where available: "~~$179.99~~ From $135.99." Comply with FTC 16 CFR Part 233.

5–15% conversion lift from adding credible strikethrough pricing. Anchoring bias is one of the strongest effects in pricing psychology.
Price Anchoring Gold
View Source
36 Medium
BOTH

Price Display

"From $X" pricing with no configuration context. Made to Order badge adds ambiguity about whether MTO carries a premium.

Recommendation

Show full price range ("$135.99 – $249.99") or name the base variant.

17% of cart-abandoning shoppers cite cost uncertainty as their reason (Baymard 2024).
Price Transparency Gold
View Source
37 Medium
BOTH

Shipping Cost Display

Free shipping banner is visible but static — no dynamic progress indicator in cart showing how close the shopper is to qualifying.

Recommendation

Add a real-time "You're $X away from FREE shipping!" progress bar in the cart drawer.

Goal-gradient research shows purchase frequency accelerates ~82% as customers approach a reward threshold. 58% of shoppers add items to reach free-shipping thresholds.
Free Shipping Gold
View Source
38 Low
BOTH

Price Display

Charm pricing (.99) applied inconsistently. Some products use .99, others use .00.

Recommendation

Audit all prices for left-digit crossing opportunities. Ensure .99 consistency in the $50–$200 range.

Small but reliable positive effect (d = 0.11) on purchase intentions. Low-cost micro-optimization.
Charm Pricing Gold
View Source
39 Low
BOTH

Discount Display

No sale badges, discount percentages, or savings callouts on any homepage product card.

Recommendation

For products on sale, show strikethrough + savings amount. Use dollar-off for items over $100.

Dollar-off framing generates higher perceived deal value for products over $100 (Hardesty & Bearden 2003).
Discount Framing Gold
View Source

BOTH

Subcategories buried in mobile-only navigation are invisible to desktop users.

Category Navigation

No BreadcrumbList schema on homepage (expected). Risk is downstream — collection and product pages need verified breadcrumb schema.

Breadcrumb schema improves CTR in SERPs by showing category hierarchy.

BOTH

Price Display

One card shows a red strikethrough "From $1,549.95" with no sale price visible — sale state may not be fully rendering.

Recommendation

For any card with a strikethrough, ensure the sale price is also visible. A crossed-out price with no replacement creates confusion.

Shoppers make pass/fail price judgments in 1–2 seconds. Ambiguous pricing causes skips.
Product Cards Gold
View Source
What’s Working Well
  • search-ux: Prominently sized search bar (929px wide, 55px tall) spans the center of the header.
  • shipping-cost-display: Free shipping threshold clearly disclosed in full-width announcement bar.
  • canonical: Self-referencing canonical correctly set to https://www.awdmods.com/.
  • logo-alt: Header logo has non-empty alt attribute.
  • CDN delivery: Shopify Fastly CDN with versioned URLs and edge caching.
  • font-preloading: Two fonts preloaded with fetchpriority="high" from Shopify fonts CDN.
E-Commerce Psychology (ECP) v5.0.1
